description: Unified dataset for blind code review study. Each question contains 4 solutions per language (2 human, 2 AI). _hidden fields must never be exposed to study participants.
uiNote: Solution order must be randomized per participant and per question. Slot labels A/B/C/D are purely positional. The _hidden key on every solution object must not be rendered in any participant-facing UI component.

questions.title: Container With Most Water
questions.problemStatement: You are given an integer array `height` of length `n`. There are `n` vertical lines drawn such that the two endpoints of the `i`-th line are `(i, 0)` and `(i, height[i])`. Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container, such that the container contains the most water. Return the maximum amount of water a container can store.

solutions.code:
def maxArea(self, height):
    # Use two pointers starting from both ends
    left_pointer = 0
    right_pointer = len(height) - 1
    maximum_water = 0

    while left_pointer < right_pointer:
        # Calculate water with current boundaries
        current_height = min(height[left_pointer], height[right_pointer])
        current_width = right_pointer - left_pointer
        current_water = current_height * current_width
        maximum_water = max(maximum_water, current_water)

        # Move the shorter boundary inward to potentially find a better solution
        if height[left_pointer] < height[right_pointer]:
            left_pointer += 1
        else:
            right_pointer -= 1

    return maximum_water

def maxArea(self, height):
    l, r, res = 0, len(height) - 1, 0
    while l < r:
        res = max(res, (r - l) * min(height[l], height[r]))
        if height[l] < height[r]: l += 1
        else: r -= 1
    return res

questions.title: Next Permutation
questions.problemStatement: A permutation of an array of integers is an arrangement of its members into a sequence or linear order. The next permutation of an array of integers is the next lexicographically greater permutation of its integer. If such arrangement is not possible, the array must be rearranged as the lowest possible order (sorted in ascending order). Implement the function in-place.

solutions.code:
def nextPermutation(self, nums):
    n = len(nums)

    # Step 1: Find the rightmost element smaller than its successor
    pivot_index = n - 2
    while pivot_index >= 0 and nums[pivot_index] >= nums[pivot_index + 1]:
        pivot_index -= 1

    if pivot_index >= 0:
        # Step 2: Find the smallest element to the right of pivot that is larger
        swap_index = n - 1
        while nums[swap_index] <= nums[pivot_index]:
            swap_index -= 1
        # Step 3: Swap pivot with that element
        nums[pivot_index], nums[swap_index] = nums[swap_index], nums[pivot_index]

    # Step 4: Reverse the suffix to get the next smallest permutation
    left, right = pivot_index + 1, n - 1
    while left < right:
        nums[left], nums[right] = nums[right], nums[left]
        left += 1
        right -= 1

def nextPermutation(self, nums):
    i = len(nums) - 2
    while i >= 0 and nums[i] >= nums[i+1]: i -= 1
    if i >= 0:
        j = len(nums) - 1
        while nums[j] <= nums[i]: j -= 1
        nums[i], nums[j] = nums[j], nums[i]
    nums[i+1:] = reversed(nums[i+1:])

questions.title: 3Sum
questions.problemStatement: Given an integer array `nums`, return all the triplets `[nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]]` such that `i != j`, `i != k`, and `j != k`, and `nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0`. Notice that the solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

solutions.code:
def threeSum(self, nums):
    nums.sort()
    res = []
    for i, v in enumerate(nums[:-2]):
        if v > 0: break
        if i > 0 and v == nums[i-1]: continue
        l, r = i+1, len(nums)-1
        while l < r:
            s = v + nums[l] + nums[r]
            if s < 0: l += 1
            elif s > 0: r -= 1
            else:
                res.append([v, nums[l], nums[r]])
                while l < r and nums[l] == nums[l+1]: l += 1
                while l < r and nums[r] == nums[r-1]: r -= 1
                l += 1; r -= 1
    return res

questions.title: Longest Palindromic Substring
questions.problemStatement: Given a string `s`, return the longest palindromic substring in `s`.
constraints: s consists of only digits and English letters

questions.title: Group Anagrams
questions.problemStatement: Given an array of strings `strs`, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An anagram is a word or phrase formed by rearranging the letters of a different word or phrase, typically using all the original letters exactly once.
constraints: strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters

questions.title: Generate Parentheses
questions.problemStatement: Given `n` pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.

questions.title: Top K Frequent Elements
questions.problemStatement: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
constraints: k is in the range [1, the number of unique elements in the array]
It is guaranteed that the answer is unique

solutions.code:
def topKFrequent(self, nums, k):
    from collections import Counter
    count = Counter(nums)
    return [x for x, _ in count.most_common(k)]

questions.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
questions.problemStatement: Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `k`, return the total number of subarrays whose sum equals to `k`. A subarray is a contiguous non-empty sequence of elements within an array.

solutions.code:
def subarraySum(self, nums, k):
    from collections import defaultdict
    count = 0
    prefix = 0
    seen = defaultdict(int)
    seen[0] = 1
    for n in nums:
        prefix += n
        count += seen[prefix - k]
        seen[prefix] += 1
    return count

def subarraySum(self, nums, k):
    from collections import defaultdict

    # prefix_count[s] = number of times prefix sum s has appeared so far
    prefix_count = defaultdict(int)
    prefix_count[0] = 1  # empty prefix has sum 0

    running_sum = 0
    valid_subarrays = 0

    for number in nums:
        running_sum += number

        # If (running_sum - k) was seen before, those subarrays sum to k
        valid_subarrays += prefix_count[running_sum - k]

        prefix_count[running_sum] += 1

    return valid_subarrays

def subarraySum(self, nums, k):
    from collections import defaultdict
    pre, res, d = 0, 0, defaultdict(int, {0: 1})
    for n in nums:
        pre += n
        res += d[pre - k]
        d[pre] += 1
    return res

questions.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
questions.problemStatement: Given a string `s`, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
constraints: s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces
